UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PETITIONERS SAMETC, LLC AND JOHN SOLLNER'S MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PETITIONERS FAILED TO (1) PROVIDE EVIDENCE WITH THEIR MOVING PAPERS THAT CONDITION OF RECONSIDERATION ORDER WAS SATISFIED; (2) CITE ANY AUTHORITY OF FACT SHOWING THAT ORDER RE: MULTIPLE ISSUES IS A FINAL AWARD AS THERE IS NO EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT ISSUES HAVE BEEN SUBMITTED TO THE ARBITRATION, WHAT ISSUES HAVE BEEN FINALLY RESOLVED AND FOR WHAT ISSUES ARBITRATOR RESERVED JURISDICTION FOR FURTHER REVIEW; (3) PROVIDE AUTHORITY OR POINT TO A CONTRACTUAL PROVISION AUTHORIZING STEVEN BLOCK TO APPOINT A SUCCESSOR ARBITRATOR. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER RESPONDENT'S SUBMISSION OF JULY 28, 2014 WHICH WAS UNTIMELY AND UNFILED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER PETITIONER'S REPLY BRIEF FILED ON JULY 29, 2014. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT AN ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.2312(B). (501/AJR/RG)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC NOTICE OF CONTINUED HEARING IS GRANTED IN PART AND REMANDED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECISION AND THE JUDGMENT THEREON CONTAINED A TEMPORARY BAR ON OWNERSHIP BASED ON THEN-EXISTING CIRCUMSTANCES. SEE FINAL AWARD AND MULTIPLE ISSUES ORDER. THE MATTER IS REMANDED TO THE ARBITRATOR TO DECIDE WHETHER THE SAME OR OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES EXIST FOR THE BAR ON OWNERSHIP TO BE MAINTAINED OR DISSOLVED. THESE ISSUES MIGHT HAVE BEEN PREVIOUSLY ADDRESSED BY THE ARBITRATOR, HOWEVER, IT MIGHT HAVE BEEN PREMATURE, AND THE ARBITRATOR SHALL REVISIT, IF NECESSARY, THE ISSUES OR MAKE A DETERMINATION BASED ON THE FACTS AS HAVE BEEN PREVIOUSLY PRESENTED IN LIGHT OF THIS DETERMINATION. THE REQUEST FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Mark Epstein Esq., Seiler Epstein LLP, 415-979-0500, in-person for petitioner
Phil Foster Esq., 415-626-8189, in-person for petitioner
Mark Clausen Esq., 415-707-235-3663, via zoom for respondent, Reginald Hindly

Mr. Foster contests the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEY'S FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is DENIED. The post-judgment motion decided by this Court and subject to the latest appellate decision, is not yet resolved as this Court was instructed to "proceed to decide whether factual and legal issues that may need to be decided in order to determine whether the bar on Epstein's ownership of the property should remain in place." The Court has not yet been presented with argument or evidence on this point. If and when this issue is resolved, upon presentation of argument and evidence, the determination of the prevailing party may be revisited. As to the determination of the prevailing party on appeal from the Court's prior decision, the Court finds that there was no prevailing party that obtained a clear and unqualified win.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reporter Jane Hong, CSR 11975, 909-831-2644, CSRJane@hotmail.com. (D501)

Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Nov-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Mark W Epstein, Esq. of Seiler Epstein Ziegler & Applegate LLP (415-979-0500), counsel for petitioner/opposing party
No Appearance by respondent/moving party

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

Respondent's Motion For New Trial is DENIED on all bases as listed in the Notice of Intention to Move for New Trial and Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Sufficient evidence was presented to the court regarding jurisdiction and binding effect of the arbitrator's decisions as to all TIC members/parties to the Four-Way Agreement. The Subdivided Lands Act issue was improperly raised for the first time at the hearing. Appeal in case 501631 has no bearing on the petition at bar. Additionally, even though respondents did not comply with the requirements of CCP 1291 and 632 in properly requesting a statement of decision ("The request for a statement of decision shall specify those controverted issues as to which the party is requesting a statement of decision."), the Court explained "the factual and legal basis for its decision" in the September 17, 2014 Amended Order re: Petition to Confirm Arbitration. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Off Calendar Jul-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark W. Epstein, Seiler, Epstein, Ziegler, & Applegate, LLP 415-979-0500 for Petitioner and moving party, Sametc, LLC and John Sollner,
Ted Elms, 415-385-9735 for Respondent, appearing in Pro Per
Phil Foster, 415-626-7744, specially appearing for Respondents, Ted and Pauline Elms.
Mr. Foster is instructed to file a substitution of attorney.
Mark Epstein, counsel for petitioners does not object to Phil Foster specially appearing for respondents Ted and Pauline Elms.

Argued: Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONERs SAMETC, LLC and JOHN SOLLNER's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is DENIED without prejudice. Petitioners failed to (1) provide evidence with their moving papers that condition of reconsideration order was satisfied; (2) cite any authority of fact showing that order re: multiple issues is a final award as there is no evidence as to what issues have been submitted to the arbitration, what issues have been finally resolved and for what issues arbitrator reserved jurisdiction for further review; (3) provide authority or point to a contractual provision authorizing Steven Block to appoint a successor arbitrator. The Court did not consider Respondent's submission of July 28, 2014 which was untimely and unfiled. The Court did not consider Petitioner's reply brief filed on July 29, 2014. Prevailing party is to prepare and submit an order pursuant to CRC 3.2312(b) = (501/AJR) -
